NKE.reasoning: Vote count: 1 bullish (mean_reversion), 1 bearish (trend_momentum), 3 neutral (breakout, catalyst_news, macro_context). This is a deeply split result with no actionable directional edge. The one bullish and one bearish voice represent a specific and important conflict: mean_reversion sees an extreme statistical extension (RSI-7 at 15.36, -14.28% below 50-SMA, confirmed daily and hourly bullish RSI divergence) and wants to fade the extreme for a bounce to the middle Bollinger Band (~$46.94); trend_momentum sees a confirmed downtrend (ADX 31.34, EMAs fanning bearish, all ROC readings negative) and wants to short into the next bounce toward $44.98–$46.16 EMA zone. These two views are both logically coherent and create a genuine coin-flip environment. Macro_context applies its asymmetry filter and finds that neither long nor short clears the 2:1 R/R minimum at current prices ($44.36): the long to $46.97 is 5.9% upside vs 5.1% downside (1.15:1), and the short to $42.09 produces an even worse ratio from current levels. Catalyst_news correctly notes there is no catalyst within 10 trading days (earnings June 25) and no constructive base. Breakout is in monitoring mode only with no volume confirmation. Wiki memory is a strong confidence dial-down: NKE has a 0W/1L track record in this account, the one closed trade was a short that lost $35.70 because deeply oversold insider-buying territory created a bounce that triggered the stop — the exact same scenario now (board-level insider buying still in the picture, RSI-7 at 15.36, mean_reversion flagging bounce probability). The lesson is clear: NKE in extreme oversold territory is a stop-hunter, not a clean entry in either direction. Current market context is broadly risk-on (S&P ATH, best April since 2020), which further undermines the short case in the short term. Correct posture: stand aside and reassess when (a) the mean-reversion bounce completes and price returns to the 21 EMA zone ($46.16) for a higher-quality short re-entry, or (b) price breaks below $42.09 on 1.5x volume for a confirmed breakdown continuation. Neither trigger is active today.
NKE.key_conflicts: The core conflict is trend_momentum (bearish, 65 confidence) vs. mean_reversion (bullish, 58 confidence). Trend_momentum: 'EMA fan is fully bearish, ADX 31.34 confirms the downtrend is real, all ROC readings are negative — short on bounces.' Mean_reversion: 'RSI-7 at 15.36 is the deepest oversold reading in the universe, confirmed bullish RSI divergence on both daily and hourly, MACD histogram turned positive — the extreme is ripe for a snapback to $46.94.' The disagreement is not a coin flip — it is telling us that the downtrend is real AND that the immediate price location is too oversold to be actionable in either direction. Macro_context settles the dispute by math: neither side clears 2:1 R/R at current prices ($44.36). The three neutral agents (breakout, catalyst_news, macro_context) are each independently saying 'wait.' Wiki memory adds a specific warning: the one NKE closed trade lost $35.70 precisely because insider buying in this oversold zone triggered a bounce that stopped out the short before the trend continued. Same configuration today (board buying still on record, RSI at 15). No trade is the high-conviction call.

DIS.reasoning: Vote count: 4 bullish (mean_reversion 52, breakout 58, catalyst_news 68, macro_context 58), 1 neutral (trend_momentum 45). Strong consensus with the lone dissenter (trend_momentum) withholding only because of a daily EMA tangle (10 EMA > 50 EMA > 21 EMA, not the canonical 10 > 21 > 50 fan) and a slightly negative 10-day ROC (-0.14%) — a technical quibble, not a bearish view. The four bullish agents all arrive at the same general entry/target/stop cluster despite approaching from different angles: entries range $102.50–$103.90 (median ~$103.75), targets cluster tightly at $107.11 (14-test resistance, also the breakout measured move target), stops cluster at $100.80–$101.02 (volume-confirmed 17-test support). This tight level convergence across four independent analytical lenses is a high-quality signal. Catalyst_news carries the highest weight in the current environment — Q2 FY2026 earnings are 4–9 trading days away (May 6–13), firmly inside the catalyst window. This is a pre-earnings momentum setup with constructive technicals: April 30 produced a strong bullish engulfing candle (+$2.45, open $100.91, close $103.75) on 1.23x above-average volume reversing from the $100.42 intraday low; daily MACD has a fresh bullish crossover; hourly EMAs are fully aligned uptrend; ADX 37.34 with +DI (32.31) well above -DI (18.74); Z-score 0.75 (not extended). Macro_context confirms 3.1:1 asymmetry math at pullback entry ($102.50 entry, $107.11 target, $101.02 stop: $4.61 upside / $1.48 downside). Risk-on macro regime (S&P ATH, Iran de-escalation) supports consumer discretionary. Wiki memory: no open DIS position (no capital deployed, no urgency penalty), and the one prior DIS signal (Apr 15 cautious buy at $100.50) was never executed because price was already at resistance — today's setup has moved further through that resistance and confirmed the break. Confidence DIAL-DOWN applied for: (1) DIS remains below EMA200 ($105.49) and SMA200 ($109.33) — longer-term institutional trend is still down, caps the bull case; (2) daily OBV is in distribution mode (hourly OBV is accumulating — a split, with the short-term picture bullish but the longer-term picture still distributing); (3) earnings binary risk — a miss vs. $1.57 EPS / $25.76B revenue would gap past the $101.02 stop; (4) volume on the breakout candle was 1.23x, below the ideal 1.5x threshold. Net confidence: 62. Entry on any intraday pullback to $102.50–$103.90 (hourly support cluster and Fib 61.8% zone); do not chase above $104.50. Stop $101.00 (hard close-basis). Target $107.11. R/R: 3.1:1 at median entry.
DIS.key_conflicts: The lone dissent comes from trend_momentum (neutral, 45 confidence), which withholds a bullish signal because: (a) daily EMAs are tangled in a non-canonical order (10 EMA $102.69 > 50 EMA $102.25 > 21 EMA $101.93), failing its strict alignment filter; (b) the 10-day ROC is -0.14%, breaking the requirement for all ROC readings to be positive. This is a precision disagreement, not a directional opposition — trend_momentum is not bearish on DIS, it is saying 'the trend structure is not clean enough to enter by my rules.' The four bullish agents explicitly acknowledge this tangle (mean_reversion notes the April 30 bullish engulfing from support; breakout highlights the coil-and-fire sequence; catalyst_news is driven by the earnings window, not the EMA alignment; macro_context's R/R math does not require a perfect EMA fan). Resolution: the EMA tangle is a quality filter for trend_momentum's specific entry type (with-trend continuation) but does not negate the breakout-from-consolidation or catalyst-driven thesis. The more material risk flagged consistently across agents is DIS still trading below its 200-day moving averages, which means the longer-term institutional trend is distribution, not accumulation — the current bullish setup is a short-term swing within a longer-term recovery, not a confirmed structural uptrend. This is priced into the 62 confidence ceiling and the tight stop below $101.02.
